patterns_recognized: Same-matchup consecutive GOLD losses: CIN@CLE and TOR@DET both lost yesterday (DET 3-2, CIN 7-6) as GOLD picks with confidence_downgrade + bullpen_fatigue stacked. Both return today with identical flag profiles — 05-15 postgame explicitly flagged monitoring the next CIN@CLE instance within 10 picks.

f5_full_delta + bullpen_fatigue DOWNGRADE rule validated on TOR@DET 05-15 (delta=0.1692, lost 3-2). Today's TOR@DET shows delta=0.2989, the largest non-Coors Stage 1/2 gap in the 2026 archive, with bullpen_fatigue confirmed again.

Jacob deGrom confirmed as CIN's probable pitcher (statsapi). The F1_xera_gap=0.99* (min_pa=65) almost certainly reflects deGrom returning from injury; thin 2026 data likely underestimates his true quality, weakening the CLE full-game thesis further.

PLATINUM→GOLD DOWNGRADE rule (f5_full_delta > 0.15 + SP edge unconfirmed in grade_reasons) validated by BOS@ATL 05-15 win. Applied today to SF@OAK: delta=0.2822 is the highest non-Coors value on any slate to date, grade_reasons explicitly note SP edge unconfirmed.

HARD CAP 1 (negative edge) continues to correctly block staking on PHI@PIT (edge=-9.41pp), MIA@TB (-4.13pp), LAD@LAA (-3.99pp); fair_prob < 0.42 floor fires on TEX@HOU (0.4105) and SD@SEA (0.3697) as designed.

TOR @ DET.reasoning: f5_full_delta=0.2989 is the largest non-Coors Stage 1/2 gap on the slate, well above the 0.15 Heuristic 5 threshold. Yesterday's identical TOR@DET matchup (delta=0.1692, bullpen_fatigue=True) ended DET 3-2. Today's even larger delta, with confidence_downgrade + bullpen_fatigue both stacked again, makes TOR's full-game thesis entirely dependent on a strained bullpen overcoming DET's dominant F5 edge. F3=4892 is real but insufficient against these compounding structural flags.
lessons_applied: TOR@DET 05-15 loss (same-matchup delta+bullpen pattern)
Heuristic 5: f5_full_delta > 0.15 + bullpen_fatigue = DOWNGRADE

KC @ STL.reasoning: Strong A- grade with SP edge (+0.40 xERA), Stage 1/2 agree (delta=0.02), and F3=1477 all supporting STL direction. Edge=+1.95pp falls just below the [4,15]pp staking band floor, correctly blocking any stake via stake_mult=0. Market prices STL at 50.81% fair_prob — essentially a coin flip despite the A- grade. CONFIRM the GOLD tier designation; the no-stake outcome is correctly applied by the edge band rule, not a tier judgment.
lessons_applied: edge band [4,15]pp floor correctly excludes staking independent of tier grade

ARI @ COL.reasoning: SP edge 2.18 xERA against COL and PQI against COL (Δ=-28.6) both correctly identify this as a model artifact. edge=+15.37pp exceeds the [4,15]pp band ceiling, adding a second staking exclusion. 05-15 postgame established that extreme Coors f5_full_delta values are park artifacts — today's delta=0.0428 is tight anyway. D-grade SKIP confirmed; the COL probability inflation is a structural Coors effect.
lessons_applied: Coors park model inflation confirmed in archive
dual negative indicators (SP edge + PQI against pick) confirm D-grade SKIP

BAL @ WSH.reasoning: F1_xera_gap=1.20* + F3=2601 satisfy HARD CAP 5's secondary-signal requirement. SP edge (+0.86 xERA) and PQI confirm WSH direction (+4.2). Edge=+6.54pp in band. f5_full_delta=0.2047 exceeds the 0.15 concern threshold, but confidence_downgrade=False and no bullpen_fatigue flag — Heuristic 5 requires confirmed overwork to mandate downgrade, which is absent here. WSH won this matchup yesterday 3-2 and today has a substantially stronger case (B+). CONFIRM.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 5: F1* + F3 secondary signal satisfies dual-signal requirement
Heuristic 5: bullpen_fatigue required to mandate DOWNGRADE on f5_full_delta > 0.15

PHI @ PIT.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 correctly fired: edge=-9.41pp caps grade to B- and zeroes the stake. Despite strong F1=3.12 and F3=2107 in PHI's direction, the deeply negative edge signals the market holds superior information about PIT's SP advantage. Per archive validation, every negative-edge GOLD/PLATINUM pick with active stake exposure has produced a loss in direction. Confirming the B-/no-stake outcome; confidence_downgrade + bullpen_fatigue stacking adds further caution.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 validated 6+ times in archive
negative edge on PLATINUM correctly caps at B- and zeros stake

MIA @ TB.reasoning: Negative edge (-4.13pp) correctly blocks staking. SP edge 0.36 xERA AGAINST TB direction and PQI against TB (Δ=-8.5) provide no directional support beyond a thin 52.16% model probability. bullpen_fatigue flagged. Yesterday TB won 7-2 as a D-grade SKIP — expected variance with no bearing on today. D-grade SKIP confirmed; no qualitative basis to upgrade.
lessons_applied: D-grade SKIP wins are expected variance, not a signal to upgrade tier
negative edge correctly blocks staking even on directional wins

CIN @ CLE.reasoning: Identical matchup to yesterday: CLE was GOLD CONFIRM and lost 7-6 to CIN. Today repeats with confidence_downgrade=True + bullpen_fatigue=True stacked. The 05-15 postgame explicitly flagged this flag combination for 10-pick monitoring — today is within that window. API confirms Jacob deGrom is CIN's probable pitcher; F1_xera_gap=0.99* (min_pa=65) underestimates deGrom's true quality, weakening the CLE thesis. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0176 indicates CIN's HL pen is marginally better. DOWNGRADE to SKIP.
lessons_applied: CIN@CLE 05-15 loss: same-matchup confidence_downgrade + bullpen_fatigue
three-flag GOLD monitoring alert from 05-15 postgame
Jacob deGrom thin-sample F1* underestimation risk (API confirmed)

TEX @ HOU.reasoning: fair_prob=0.4105 < 0.42 floor correctly triggers automatic SKIP regardless of PLATINUM tier. SP edge 0.65 xERA AGAINST HOU direction and PQI against HOU (Δ=-12.0) both favor TEX structurally. Model's HOU probability (50.49%) barely exceeds coin flip while market prices TEX as the stronger side. D-grade with fair_prob below floor is a hard stop. No qualitative basis to override.
lessons_applied: fair_prob < 0.42 floor = automatic SKIP regardless of tier
SP edge + PQI both against pick = D-grade confirmed

CHC @ CHW.reasoning: edge=+1.18pp outside [4,15]pp band and D-grade correctly exclude. SP edge and PQI both run against CHC direction; no F-signal fires for CHC. Model barely picks CHC at 50.25% — essentially a coin flip. Yesterday CHC demolished CHW 10-5 as a D-grade SKIP: that blowout is expected variance and provides no predictive basis for today.
lessons_applied: no F-signal fire on SKIP pick = correct non-staking signal
edge below band floor correctly excludes
D-grade direction wins are variance, not tier recalibration signals

MIL @ MIN.reasoning: Three-signal GOLD bar cleanly met: F3=754 (> 500 threshold), edge=+8.42pp in [4,15]pp band, f5_full_delta=0.077 < 0.15, confidence_downgrade=False, no bullpen_fatigue. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0003 (neutral pen comparison). pen_strain=0.095 (MODERATE). The bar is 4-for-5 in the archive — the sole loss (CIN@CLE 05-15) had three stacked flags absent here. Clean CONFIRM.
lessons_applied: three-signal GOLD bar (F3 > 500 + edge > 4pp + delta < 0.15 + no flags) validated across archive
absence of stacked flags distinguishes from the 05-15 first-loss pattern

SD @ SEA.reasoning: fair_prob=0.3697 is well below the 0.42 floor, triggering automatic SKIP. Market prices SEA (home) at only 37%, yet the model barely gives SD 50.79%. SP edge and PQI both run against SD direction. Yesterday SEA was picked and lost 2-0 to SD (HARD CAP 1 correctly blocked stake). Today SD is the model pick but market structure dominates at this extreme fair_prob gap.
lessons_applied: fair_prob < 0.42 floor fires correctly at 0.37
market pricing at extreme levels dominates D-grade model direction

BOS @ ATL.reasoning: edge=+3.82pp falls just below the [4,15]pp band floor, correctly blocking staking. SP edge 0.91 xERA AGAINST ATL (small-sample BOS SP, min_pa=87) and PQI against ATL (Δ=-7.8) run counter to the pick despite F2+F3 firing. Yesterday's BOS@ATL was a validated PLATINUM→GOLD DOWNGRADE (ATL won 3-2); today's edge is narrower and falls outside the band entirely. C-grade CONFIRM of SKIP designation.
lessons_applied: BOS@ATL 05-15 PLATINUM→GOLD DOWNGRADE validated (direction win)
edge below band floor correctly excludes staking even on PLATINUM

NYY @ NYM.reasoning: API confirms Carlos Rodón (NYY, probable) has 0 Statcast pitches season-to-date, validating PENDING_SP_DATA. Without SP quality data for Rodón, the model cannot reliably score the matchup. This is the seventh application of the PENDING_SP_DATA protocol in the 2026 archive — all six prior instances produced correct no-pick outcomes regardless of final score. No qualitative basis to override a protocol with 6-for-6 accuracy.
lessons_applied: PENDING_SP_DATA protocol validated 6 times in archive
Carlos Rodón 0 Statcast pitches confirmed via statsapi API

LAD @ LAA.reasoning: Negative edge (-3.99pp) and acute_roster + bullpen_fatigue both flagged. Market prices LAA at 56.52% (fair_prob) while model only gives LAD 52.53% — negative edge signals market has better information about LAA's structural advantage. f5_full_delta=0.1549 approaches the 0.15 concern threshold. Yesterday LAD won 6-0 as a SKIP pick (expected variance). C-grade SKIP confirmed.
lessons_applied: negative edge + acute_roster correctly produces SKIP outcome
market asymmetry (fair_prob vs model gap) dominates on C-grade edge-negative picks

SF @ OAK.reasoning: f5_full_delta=0.2822 is the highest non-Coors Stage 1/2 gap on the slate, well above the 0.15 PLATINUM→GOLD DOWNGRADE threshold. Grade_reasons explicitly state 'SP edge unconfirmed' — both conditions for the validated rule are met. OAK wins only 34.6% of F5 innings; the full-game 62.8% thesis rests entirely on OAK's bullpen, and bullpen_fatigue=True + confidence_downgrade=True are stacked. Yesterday's OAK PLATINUM win had delta=0.0985 (3x tighter, no fatigue flags). Direction intact — GOLD.
lessons_applied: PLATINUM→GOLD DOWNGRADE: f5_full_delta > 0.15 + SP edge unconfirmed validated by BOS@ATL 05-15
bullpen_fatigue + confidence_downgrade on full-game-bullpen-dependent thesis warrants tier reduction
